heading: Ali Curung vs Alpurrurulam — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Ali Curung, Northern Territory and Alpurrurulam, Northern Territory. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Ali Curung has a lower overall climate risk score (31/100) compared to Alpurrurulam (35/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Ali Curung or Alpurrurulam safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Ali Curung has a flood risk score of 28/100, while Alpurrurulam scores 49/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Ali Curung scores 40/100 for bushfire risk. Alpurrurulam scores 26/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Ali Curung or Alpurrurulam?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Ali Curung has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.8% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
